stdout:
Usage: gaia build compile [OPTIONS] [PATH]

 Compile a knowledge package to ``.gaia/ir.json``.

 Loads the package's Python DSL, applies any sidecar priors (``priors.py``),
 lowers it into the canonical IR v2 JSON, runs the IR validator, and
 writes ``.gaia/ir.json`` + ``.gaia/ir_hash`` +
 ``.gaia/compile_metadata.json``.
 Downstream verbs (``gaia run infer``, ``gaia run render``, ``gaia inspect
 starmap``, ``gaia pkg register``) all require fresh compile artifacts.

 Example:

 .. code-block:: bash

     gaia build compile .

в•­в”Ђ Arguments в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв•®

в”‚ --sync-host    --no-sync-host      When the host has its own pyproject.toml, в”‚
в”‚                                    run `uv sync` against it before importing в”‚
в”‚                                    the Gaia package. Off by default in the   в”‚
в”‚                                    embedded layout вЂ” the host's environment  в”‚
в”‚                                    is none of Gaia's business unless the     в”‚
в”‚                                    user opts in. Always on in the legacy     в”‚
в”‚                                    layout (a legacy host's pyproject IS the  в”‚
в”‚                                    Gaia package and its deps must be         в”‚
в”‚                                    installed for the import to succeed).     в”‚
в”‚                                    [default: no-sync-host]                   в”‚
в”‚ --help                             Show this message and exit.               в”‚
